body.text: Tuesday open: DOJ's Colorado AR-15 and magazine-ban lawsuits become the week's dominant 2A storyline; ATF's new interstate-transport NPRM is the highest member-relevance consumer rule of the package; Monday's SCOTUS orders list shifts to recap mode

Three storylines anchor the open. First, the DOJ's two-pronged Colorado lawsuit — against Denver's 1989 'assault weapons' ordinance (filed May 6) and Colorado's post-Aurora magazine-capacity ban (filed May 7) — is the splashiest legal intervention to date by DOJ's new Second A...

2. ATF Section 926A interstate-transport NPRM — new consumer r…
3. ATF rule package — comment Week 2 (brace + EITB)
